  • macOS

  • Windows

    Experimental support. Keep the app in the foreground to improve relay stability.

Note: Desktop builds are less suggested for regular use. Prefer Android for Bluetooth Support

What is Yatra Mesh?

Yatra Mesh is a peer-to-peer messaging app using wifi and bluetooth to create a decentralized mesh network. All messages are end-to-end encrypted for your privacy.

  • Wifi Mesh

    Connect to the local “Yatra Mesh” hotspot or ad-hoc network. Devices pass messages peer-to-peer without a central server.

  • Bluetooth Relay

    When wifi isn’t available, nearby devices can hand off messages via bluetooth, extending reach through the crowd.

  • Experimental & Community

    Made by open source developers as free(dom) software. Expect quirks. Share feedback.

FAQ

How do I join the mesh?
Install the Android app, enable bluetooth and wifi, then open Yatra Mesh. If a local hotspot or ad-hoc SSID appears (e.g., “Yatra Mesh”), connect to it. The app will announce and relay messages to nearby peers automatically.
Is my data private?
Messages are relayed peer-to-peer and can be end-to-end encrypted when chatting directly. In mesh relays, metadata like relay hops may be visible to participating nodes. Avoid sensitive information; this is an experimental network.
Why Android over desktop?
Android supports background scanning/relaying with better power profiles and direct bluetooth/wifi APIs. Desktop platforms require more permissions, have background limitations, and are best used as relay nodes.
Troubleshooting tips
Ensure bluetooth and wifi are enabled. Keep the app in foreground when first joining. Move closer to the crowd. If you lose messages, toggle airplane mode off/on to refresh radios, or restart the app to reannounce presence.
Event Schedule
Back to Home

Four days of mesh experiments, workshops, and live relays. Bring your Android device and curiosity.

Fri 27th
  • 18:00 • Mesh Opens

    Mesh network live. Download Android app on site. Mesh onboarding and vibe check.

  • 19:30 • Mesh Warmup

    Ad-hoc network spin-up and first relay tests across the venue.

  • 21:00 • Mesh Mixer

    Group chats, message relays, and scavenger prompts through the mesh.

Sat 28th
  • 12:00 • Workshop: Mesh Basics

    Hands-on intro to peer-to-peer messaging, bluetooth relays, and wifi ad-hoc networks.

  • 15:00 • Mesh Quest

    A venue-wide relay challenge with checkpoints, puzzles, and message hops.

  • 20:00 • Night Mesh Jam

    Live visuals and message streams curated through the mesh. Bring your neon.

Sun 1st
  • 11:00 • Community Clinic

    Bring issues, ideas, and tips. We’ll help you get the best experience together.

  • 14:00 • Privacy in P2P

    Talk and Q&A on encryption, metadata, and safe use in experimental meshes.

  • 17:30 • Mesh Stories

    Share experiences from the field: what relayed, what failed, and what was magical.

Mon 2nd
  • 13:30 • Field Test

    Outdoor mesh range test and public-space relays. Measure hops and latency.

  • 16:30 • Closing Circle

    Wrap-up, feedback, and where the mesh goes next. Thanks for relaying!
